[[commons:Category:Ishimaki-jinja]][[ja:石巻神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石巻神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ishimaki Shrine | native_name = 石巻神社 | image = Ishimaki-jinja Kami Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ishimaki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.789155|137.455951}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|ca|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|es|Ōkuninushi|fa|اوکونینوشی|fr|Ōkuninushi|hu|Ókuninusi|id|Ookuninushi|it|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|ko|오오쿠니누시|mni|ꯑꯣꯀꯨꯅꯤꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Ōkuninushi|pt|Ookuninushi|ru|Окунинуси|simple|Ōkuninushi|sr|Okuninuši|sv|Ōkuninushi|tr|Ōkuninushi|uk|Окунінуші|vi|Ōkuninushi|zh|大國主|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ishimaki Shrine'''|石巻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mikawa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q336431|ltq=Q336431|qid=Q336431|1=Toyohashi}} * {{ill|Mikawa Province|lt=Mikawa Province|ar|مقاطعة ميكاوا|ckb|پارێزگای میکاوا|cs|Provincie Mikawa|de|Provinz Mikawa|en|Mikawa Province|eo|Provinco Mikaŭa|es|Provincia de Mikawa|fa|ولایت میکاوا|fi|Mikawan provinssi|fr|Province de Mikawa|gl|Provincia de Mikawa|hak|Mikawa-koet|id|Provinsi Mikawa|it|Mikawa (provincia)|ja|三河国|ko|미카와국|lt|Mikavos žemė|nl|Mikawa (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Mikawa|pt|Província de Mikawa|ru|Микава (провинция)|simple|Mikawa Province|sk|Mikawa|sr|Провинција Микава|th|แคว้นมิกาวะ|tr|Mikawa (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мікава|ur|میکاوا صوبہ|zh_classical|三河國|zh_min_nan|Mikawa-kok|zh_yue|三河國|zh|三河國|qid=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}} * {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3180305002563 == Commons category (P373) == * Ishimaki-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Ishimaki Shrine (Shimosha)|lt=Ishimaki Shrine (Shimosha)|qid=Q135190221|1=Ishimaki Shrine (Shimosha)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山上社：愛知県豊橋市石巻町南山93-2 里宮社：愛知県豊橋市石巻町金割1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県豊橋市石巻町', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 石巻神社 {{commons category|Category:Ishimaki-jinja}} [[JA:石巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11586490}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Ishimaki Shrine | 画像=[[画像:Ishimaki-jinja Kami Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Upper shrine (okumiya) haiden | 所在地=Upper shrine (okumiya): {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyohashi|Q336431|qid=Q336431|1=Toyohashi}}{{ill|lt=Ishimaki-chō (Toyohashi)|Q28682750|qid=Q28682750|lt=Ishimaki-chō|1=Ishimaki-chō}} Minamiyama 93-2
Lower shrine (satomiya): Kanewari 1, Ishimaki-chō, Toyohashi, Aichi | 位置=Upper shrine (okumiya):
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|21|N|137|27|22|E|display=inline,title}}
Lower shrine (satomiya):
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|37.8|N|137|26|46.2|E|display=inline}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} | 創建=Unknown | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
Mikawa Province Fourth Shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 本殿= | 例祭=first Sunday of April (Oni Festival) |地図={{Location map|Japan Aichi|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|label=Ishimaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=210|float=center}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.790935|N|137.450725|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|47|21|N|137|27|22|E}}|label1=Upper shrine (okumiya)|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|47|37.8|N|137|26|46.2|E}}|label2=Lower shrine (satomiya)|label-pos2=top|mark-size2=8}} }} [[画像:Ishimaki-jinja Shimo Shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Lower shrine (satomiya) haiden]] '''Ishimaki Shrine''' (いしまきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ishimaki-chō (Toyohashi)|Q28682750|qid=Q28682750|lt=Ishimaki-chō|1=Ishimaki-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Toyohashi|Q336431|qid=Q336431|1=Toyohashi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Ishimaki Shrine of Yana District, {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|qid=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}}). Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and it is regarded as the fourth shrine of Mikawa Province{{要出典|date=April 2014}}. === Overview === The founding period is unknown. It is also said to date to the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|qid=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}} or {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Ishimaki|Q11586461|qid=Q11586461|1=Mount Ishimaki}} itself is presumed to have been an object of worship. Because it lay on the northeast demon gate of {{ill|lt=Yoshida Castle (Mikawa)|Q849190|qid=Q849190|lt=Yoshida Castle|1=Yoshida Castle (Mikawa Province)}}, it is said to have received devotion from successive castle lords since the castle’s construction. It is divided between the okumiya (upper shrine) on the mid-slope of Mount Ishimaki and the satomiya (lower shrine) in the mountain village. The upper shrine holds the Kuda-gayu rite, and the lower shrine holds the Oni festival. === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Festivals === === Kuda-gayu Festival === A rite held at the Ishimaki Shrine okumiya on lunar January 15 (early February in the solar calendar) to divine agricultural fortune. The priest recites norito, lights the ritual firewood at the hearth with flint, and cooks porridge in a pot. Eighteen reed tubes labeled with crop names are placed into the boiling porridge and stirred with {{ill|lt=sakaki|Q775470|qid=Q775470|1=Cleyera japonica}} branches. After about one hour, the tubes are removed and crop fortune is divined from the state of porridge inside each tube. === Oni Festival === Annual festival held at the satomiya on the first Sunday of April. Also called the “chimaki festival” or “doburoku festival.” It is said to have begun when an oni mask was dedicated in thanks for prayers for recovery of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. Worshippers are served {{ill|lt=chimaki|Q713466|qid=Q713466|1=Zongzi}} and {{ill|lt=doburoku|Q1371541|qid=Q1371541|1=Doburoku}}. The oni of this festival are a black oni and a red oni, regarded as divine manifestations. They rampage in the precincts and scatter white powder containing candy; receiving the powder is said to prevent colds. If one fails to evade the oni, one may be showered with a large amount of powder from a can. === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Toyohashi Station|Q771465|qid=Q771465|1=Toyohashi Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|qid=Q11235139|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}}, {{ill|lt=Iida Line|Q771305|qid=Q771305|1=Iida Line}}, {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|qid=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|qid=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}), take {{ill|lt=Toyotetsu Bus|Q11634286|qid=Q11634286|1=Toyotetsu Bus}} Toyohashi-Wada-tsuji line and get off at “Ishimaki Tozanguchi.” === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Ishimaki-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ishimaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ishimaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ishimaki Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Toyohashi, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shi-no-Miya]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyohashi]]
